label: p as in pig
howTo: Press your lips together, then pop them open with a quiet puff. No voice — just air.
steps: Press both lips firmly together.
Build up a little air behind your lips.
Pop them open with a quiet puff — no voice, just air.
handCue: Hold a finger to your closed lips, then flick it away as they pop open.

label: b as in ball
manner: A pop of air with your voice on
howTo: Press your lips together and turn your voice on as they pop open. Feel your throat buzz.
steps: Press both lips together.
Turn your voice on so your throat buzzes.
Pop your lips open to let the sound out.
handCue: Bounce a loose fist near your lips as they pop, to show the voice is on.

label: m as in mum
howTo: Keep your lips gently closed and hum. The sound comes out your nose — feel your lips tickle.
steps: Close your lips gently — don't squeeze.
Turn your voice on and hum.
Feel the sound buzz out your nose and tickle your lips.
handCue: Stroke a flat hand smoothly down past your closed lips for the long hum.

label: w as in water
manner: A smooth glide with your voice on
howTo: Round your lips like a small kiss, voice on, then glide into the next sound.
steps: Round your lips into a small 'kiss' shape.
Turn your voice on.
Glide smoothly straight into the next sound.
handCue: Make a small 'O' with finger and thumb, then sweep your hand forward in a wave.

label: h as in hat
howTo: Open your mouth and let a soft breath out, like fogging up a window.
steps: Open your mouth in a relaxed, neutral shape.
Let a soft breath of air flow out — no voice.
Think of fogging up a window or a cold mirror.
handCue: Open a flat hand in front of your mouth and push it out gently with the breath.

label: t as in top
howTo: Tap your tongue tip on the bumpy spot behind your top teeth, then release a quiet tap. No voice.
steps: Lift your tongue tip to the bumpy spot behind your top teeth.
Build up a little air behind it.
Release with a quick, quiet tap — no voice.
handCue: Tap a fingertip once against the back of your top teeth, then flick it away.

label: d as in dog
manner: A tap with your voice on
howTo: Tap your tongue tip behind your top teeth with your voice on. Feel the buzz.
steps: Lift your tongue tip behind your top teeth.
Turn your voice on so your throat buzzes.
Release with a quick tap to let the sound out.
handCue: Tap a fingertip near your top teeth, with a little bounce to show the voice is on.

label: n as in no
howTo: Rest your tongue tip behind your top teeth and hum out your nose.
steps: Rest your tongue tip behind your top teeth.
Turn your voice on and hum.
Feel the sound come out your nose.
handCue: Rest a fingertip on the side of your nose to show the hum coming through it.

label: k as in car
manner: A pop of air at the back
howTo: Lift the back of your tongue to the roof at the back, then pop it open. No voice.
steps: Lift the back of your tongue up to the roof, near the back.
Build up a little air behind it.
Pop it open with a quiet release — no voice.
handCue: Point a finger back towards your throat, then flick it forward for the back pop.

label: g as in go
manner: A back pop with your voice on
howTo: Lift the back of your tongue and pop it open with your voice on. Feel the buzz.
steps: Lift the back of your tongue up to the roof at the back.
Turn your voice on so your throat buzzes.
Pop it open to let the sound out.
handCue: Point a finger towards your throat and bounce it forward to show the voice is on.

label: f as in fish
howTo: Rest your top teeth on your bottom lip and blow a long, quiet stream of air.
steps: Rest your top teeth gently on your bottom lip.
Blow a long, steady stream of air through the gap.
Keep it quiet — no voice, just air.
handCue: Lay a fingertip across your bottom lip, then draw it forward for the long airflow.

label: s as in sun
manner: A long, quiet stream of air
howTo: Lift your tongue tip near the bumpy spot behind your top teeth and hiss a long, quiet stream of air, like a snake.
steps: Lift your tongue tip close to the bumpy spot behind your top teeth.
Keep your teeth almost together and smile a little.
Hiss a long, steady stream of air — no voice, like a snake.
handCue: Trace a long, wavy line forward with one finger, like a slithering snake.

label: z as in zoo
manner: A long stream of air with your voice on
howTo: Make the same shape as 's', but turn your voice on so it buzzes like a bee.
steps: Lift your tongue tip close to the bumpy spot behind your top teeth.
Keep your teeth almost together.
Turn your voice on and let it buzz — like a busy bee.
handCue: Trace a wavy line forward while wiggling your finger, like a buzzing bee.

label: l as in lion
manner: Voice on, air flows round the tongue
howTo: Press your tongue tip up behind your top teeth, voice on, and let the sound flow around the sides — a long 'lll'.
steps: Press your tongue tip up onto the bumpy spot behind your top teeth.
Turn your voice on and keep it going.
Let the sound flow around the sides of your tongue — 'lll'.
handCue: Point your finger up and hold it steady, like your tongue tip lifting up.

label: y as in yes
manner: A smooth glide with your voice on
howTo: Start in a wide 'ee' smile with your voice on, then glide straight into the next sound.
steps: Smile wide and lift your tongue high, like starting an 'ee'.
Turn your voice on.
Glide smoothly straight into the next sound — 'yuh'.
handCue: Hold a smiling 'ee' hand near your mouth, then sweep it forward into the next sound.

label: ng as in sing
howTo: Lift the back of your tongue to the roof at the back, voice on, and hum out your nose.
steps: Lift the back of your tongue up to the roof at the back.
Turn your voice on and hum.
Feel the sound come out your nose — 'ng'.
handCue: Point a finger towards your throat, then up to your nose to show the hum.

label: ah as in cot
howTo: Open your mouth wide like the doctor's 'aah' and turn your voice on.
steps: Drop your jaw and open your mouth wide.
Turn your voice on and say 'aah'.
Think of the sound at the doctor's.
handCue: Open your hand wide below your chin, like your jaw dropping open.

label: ee as in see
howTo: Smile wide and say 'eee' with your voice on, like a happy smile sound.
steps: Stretch your lips into a wide smile.
Lift your tongue high toward the front.
Turn your voice on and say 'eee'.
handCue: Stretch a finger across your smile from one corner to the other.

label: oo as in moon
howTo: Round your lips into a small circle and say 'ooo' with your voice on.
steps: Round your lips into a small circle and push them forward.
Turn your voice on.
Say a long 'ooo'.
handCue: Make a small round 'O' with your fingers and push it forward from your lips.

label: oh as in go
howTo: Round your lips and say 'ohh' with your voice on, like a surprised 'oh!'.
steps: Round your lips into an 'O' shape.
Turn your voice on and say 'ohh'.
Think of a surprised 'oh!'.
handCue: Make a round 'O' shape with your whole hand in front of your mouth.

label: ih as in sit
howTo: Open your mouth a little, keep it relaxed, and say a short 'ih' with your voice on.
steps: Open your mouth just a little, lips relaxed.
Turn your voice on.
Say a short, quick 'ih'.
handCue: Pinch finger and thumb a small gap apart to show the short, relaxed sound.

label: eh as in bed
howTo: Open your mouth halfway and say 'eh' with your voice on, like the start of 'egg'.
steps: Open your mouth about halfway.
Turn your voice on.
Say a short 'eh', like the start of 'egg'.
handCue: Open a flat hand halfway below your chin, not as wide as 'ah'.

label: ae as in cat
manner: Voice on, mouth open and wide
howTo: Open your mouth and stretch your lips wide, then say 'aaa' with your voice on, like a happy cat.
steps: Open your mouth and stretch your lips out wide.
Turn your voice on.
Say a stretched 'aaa', like in 'cat'.
handCue: Open your hand wide and stretch it sideways below your chin.

label: uh as in cup
howTo: Let your mouth fall relaxed and slightly open, and say a short 'uh' with your voice on.
steps: Let your jaw and lips fall relaxed, mouth slightly open.
Turn your voice on.
Say a short, easy 'uh'.
handCue: Let a relaxed open hand drop a little below your chin for the easy 'uh'.
